title: Crunchy Tunisian Masarepa Fritters
description: A crunchy and energizing Tunisian dish featuring white masarepa, herbs, and spices, perfect for a paleo-friendly snack. These fritters are fried to a golden brown and packed with flavor.

ingredients.name: chicken egg
ingredients.notes: or flax egg for vegan option

instructions: In a large bowl, mix together the masarepa, egg, parsley, cilantro, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and black pepper until well combined.
Gradually add water to the mixture, stirring with a fork until the dough comes together and forms a ball.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes until it becomes pliable and smooth.
Divide the dough into 8-10 equal pieces and shape each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk shape, about 1/2 inch thick.
Heat the coconut oil in a deep frying pan over medium-high heat until it reaches 350°F.
Carefully place 2-3 fritters into the hot oil and fry for 3-4 minutes on each side, or until golden brown.
Remove the fritters from the oil with a slotted spoon and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Repeat the frying process with the remaining fritters.
Serve the Crunchy Tunisian Masarepa Fritters hot and enjoy!
